llm_result.summary: Six modifiable levers across social, cognitive-behavioral, and affective domains address the core processes described: social avoidance (C01), belief of being annoying (C02), post-event rumination (C03), plan cancellations (C04), loneliness (C05), and self-criticism after loneliness (C06). The levers target both proximal mechanisms (e.g., actual social exposure, cognitive beliefs, rumination regulation, self-compassion) and distal outcomes (enhanced belonging, reduced avoidance, more consistent social engagement). Each predictor is actionable, measurable, and designed to yield safe, scalable improvements with clear metrics and short- to medium-term horizons. Safety considerations emphasize monitoring distress during exposure and ensuring flexible options to preserve well-being.

solutions.predictor_variable: Graded social exposure with scheduling and accountability support
solutions.solution_candidate: Structured 4–8 week graded social exposure plan: start with brief social invitations, progress to small-group attendance, and finish with reflection after each exposure. Use an accountability buddy and a simple scheduling/app to reduce avoidance (C01) and cancellations (C04), while increasing meaningful social contact to alleviate loneliness (C05).
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Directly increases real-world social contact and positive experiences, builds confidence in social performance, reduces reliance on rumination, and enhances perceived belonging—producing downstream reductions in loneliness and plan-cancellation behaviors.
solutions.measurement: Weekly tally of invitations accepted/attended; track cancellations; loneliness via brief single-item or UCLA Loneliness scale; plan adherence checks.
solutions.safety_notes: Monitor for social anxiety flare during initial exposures; provide coping scripts and a quick pause option; offer crisis resources if distress escalates.
solutions.mapping_query: Maps to C01, C04, C05; aims to increase real social contact and perceived connection, reducing avoidance and loneliness.

solutions.predictor_variable: Cognitive restructuring to challenge 'I am annoying' belief
solutions.solution_candidate: CBT-based cognitive reappraisal: weekly sessions or self-guided exercises to gather evidence for/against the belief, perform behavioral experiments in real social contexts, and develop adaptive self-statements to replace harsh self-views.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Modifies core self-evaluation, lowers anticipated negative evaluation, reduces avoidance (C01) and post-event rumination (C03) by aligning beliefs with observed social experiences.
solutions.measurement: Belief strength scale (e.g., social evaluation beliefs); frequency of social engagements; post-event rumination duration (self-report).
solutions.safety_notes: Maintain supportive framing; monitor for increased social worry during initial experiments; provide escalation if distress worsens.
solutions.mapping_query: Targets C02 with potential secondary effects on C01 and C03 as beliefs shift.

solutions.predictor_variable: Rumination regulation via timeboxing and metacognitive strategies
solutions.solution_candidate: Daily rumination timeboxing (e.g., 15–20 minutes) to process concerns; outside the window, use deferral techniques, brief mindfulness/breathing, and journaling; reduces the duration and impact of post-event rumination (C03) and may ease loneliness (C05) and self-criticism (C06.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Limits repetitive negative thinking after social events, decreases distress, and prevents spillover into mood and social avoidance, thereby reducing loneliness and harsh self-criticism.
solutions.measurement: Average daily rumination duration; post-event rumination duration; loneliness and mood ratings.
solutions.safety_notes: Check for any escalation of distress; adjust timeboxing if rumination worsens.
solutions.mapping_query: Targets C03; potential downstream effects on C05 and C06 via mood stabilization.

solutions.predictor_variable: Self-compassion training to reduce self-criticism after loneliness
solutions.solution_candidate: Daily 5–10 minute self-compassion practice (guided audio) plus compassionate self-talk journaling after loneliness episodes; cultivate inner supportive dialogue to reduce self-criticism (C06) and indirectly improve mood and social engagement (C01/C05).
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Increases internal soothing, reduces harsh self-criticism, improves mood, and lowers avoidance, facilitating more approach toward social opportunities and reducing loneliness.
solutions.measurement: Self-criticism scale (FSCRS); loneliness scale; avoidance frequency; mood rating.
solutions.safety_notes: No known safety concerns; ensure language is supportive and non-judgmental.
solutions.mapping_query: Directly targets C06 with secondary effects on C01 and C05 through improved mood and self-worth.

solutions.solution_candidate: Link with community organizations, schedule group activities aligned with interests, and support ongoing participation; monitor sense of belonging and social connectedness and track attendance and quality of connections.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Increases social belonging and peer support, directly reducing loneliness and indirectly reducing avoidance by enriching rewarding social experiences.
solutions.measurement: Loneliness scale; number and quality of attended activities; perceived belonging scale.
solutions.safety_notes: Ensure group safety and optionality; allow easy opt-out if uncomfortable; provide stepwise progression to different groups.
solutions.mapping_query: Targets C05 primarily; potential indirect effects on C01 via increased engagement.

solutions.predictor_variable: Commitment devices and planning to reduce cancellations
solutions.solution_candidate: Implementation of commitment contracts (public or clinician-backed), reminders, and if-then planning for social plans; provide flexible rescheduling options to prevent distress from cancellations; track weekly cancellations and attendance.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Increases follow-through on social plans, reduces avoidance and plan cancellations, creates routine social contact, and mitigates loneliness through consistency.
solutions.measurement: Cancellation frequency per week; attendance rate; perceived plan reliability; satisfaction with social plans.
solutions.safety_notes: Maintain flexibility to reschedule without penalty if distress arises; avoid punitive contingencies.
solutions.mapping_query: Targets C01 and C04; downstream effects on C05 expected via more consistent social contact.
